findings.evidence: PR line 92 states `f x - f T = ∫ t in x..T, -iteratedDerivWithin 1 f (Icc x T) t`; Mathlib has `integral_derivWithin_Icc_of_contDiffOn_Icc` at `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/IntervalIntegral/ContDiff.lean:52`, and `iteratedDerivWithin_one` is available at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/IteratedDeriv/Defs.lean:113`.
findings.fix: Use `intervalIntegral.integral_derivWithin_Icc_of_contDiffOn_Icc` with the `ContDiffOn ℝ 1 f (Icc x T)` proof, then rewrite by `iteratedDerivWithin_one` and `intervalIntegral.integral_neg`; keep only a thin wrapper if the CM-namespaced statement is needed.
findings.issue: `IsCompletelyMonotone.integral_neg_deriv` reproves the standard FTC identity for `derivWithin` on `Icc x T`, specialized to completely monotone functions and negated.

summary: One public FTC lemma duplicates an existing Mathlib interval-integral API up to a small rewrite; the rest of the searched Chafaï/Prokhorov infrastructure did not show direct replacements.
